claim_text: For accuracy, smaller KANs can achieve comparable or better accuracy than larger MLPs in function fitting tasks.
reason: 2D: Best KAN RMSE=4.5493e-02 (150 params) vs best MLP RMSE=3.7790e-02 (10501 params)

claim_text: Theoretically and empirically, KANs possess faster neural scaling laws than MLPs.
reason: 2D: KAN exponent=-0.733 vs MLP exponent=-0.578; 4D: KAN=0.157 vs MLP=-0.209
expected_value: KAN exponent steeper (more negative) than MLP

claim_text: KANs can learn both the compositional structure and the univariate functions quite well, hence outperforming MLPs by a large margin.
Despite this suboptimality, KANs still have much better scaling laws than MLPs.
KANs can almost saturate the fastest scaling law predicted by our theory (α = 4), while MLPs scales slowly and plateau quickly.
We plot test RMSE as a function of the number of parameters for KANs and MLPs, showing that KANs have better scaling curves than MLPs.
KANs can almost saturate the steeper red lines, while MLPs struggle to converge.
KANs are more efficient and accurate in representing special functions than MLPs.
reason: 2D test: KAN best RMSE=4.5493e-02 vs MLP=3.7790e-02

claim_text: KANs' performance is shown to be consistently better than MLPs.
reason: 2D test: KAN best RMSE=4.5493e-02 vs MLP=3.7790e-02

claim_text: A 2-Layer width-10 KAN is 100 times more accurate than a 4-Layer width-100 MLP (10^-7 vs 10^-5 MSE) and 100 times more parameter efficient.
reason: KAN: 300 params, MSE=1.1760e-03; MLP: 30701 params, MSE=6.5798e-04; accuracy ratio=0.6x, param ratio=102.3x
